# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that caches dependencies and build outputs to speed up the CI pipeline. Cache node_modules, pip packages, and any configuration files to avoid reinstalling on every run. Include environment-specific configs in the cache for convenience.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Build Caching Workflow
on: [push]
jobs: 
  build_and_cache:   # job name (i.e., workflow runs on push event)
    if: startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags') == false     # only run when not a tag push
    uses: ./.github/workflows/actions-runner-setup.yml  # Runner setup as CodeSPaces的一部分 (optional). Remove it for local runner or if you have already created the GITHUB_WORKFLOW folder on your own machine in Codespaces environments where not required
    with:  
      runners-to-keep: '10'  # Set of Runner names to keep, default is top 3. Remove it for all available setups if you want latest runner or specific ones (i.e., workers) without removing the rest from here after new setup will be added later on
    env:   # Environment variables that can change settings across runs as environment variable values and not secret parameters
      RUNNER_NAME: ${{runnerName}} 
    
    with:      
        key1: value1           # Add or replace these keys to set the context for this job in workflow files. Replace them according your need, ie., 'key' would be replaced by name of a specific configuration file you have like '.env','.dockerconfigjson','tokenfile'' etc which are already provided
        key2: value2            # Add or replace these keys to set the context for this job in workflow files. Replace them according your need, ie., 'key' would be replaced by name of a specific configuration file you have like '.env','.dockerconfigjson','tokenfile'' etc which are already provided
    secrets:   # Secrets that can change settings across runs and the repositories where it is set. The values for these keys should not contain sensitive information such as passwords or tokens, those need to be stored securely (for example using github secret manager). Replace them according your needs with appropriate details
        SECRET_KEY: ${{secrets.SECRET-VALUE}}  # Add/replace the key and value for a specific repository if it's sensitive data, use secrets or other methods to store this information where needed without exposing directly in files (like commented sections below) ie., 'secretKey would be replaced by some secret details stored securely on github
        ANOTHER_SECRET: ${{secrets.ANOTHER-VALUE}} # Add/replace the key and value for a specific repository if it's sensitive data, use secrets or other methods to store this information where needed without exposing directly in files (like commented sections below) ie., 'anotherSecret would be replaced by some secret details stored securely on github
